kuala-lumpur.title: Kuala Lumpur
kuala-lumpur.tagline: Towers, temples, and the world's best street food
kuala-lumpur.gettingThere: Fly into KLIA (KUL). KLIA Ekspres train to KL Sentral takes 28 minutes (RM55). Grab taxis widely available.
highlights: Petronas Twin Towers & KLCC Park — 452m twin icons, free park and lake below
Batu Caves — 272-step climb to a Hindu temple inside a limestone cavern (free)
Jalan Alor night market hawker street — the best open-air eating street in KL
Petaling Street Chinatown — bargain market under red lanterns, 24-hour activity
Bukit Bintang shopping district — Pavilion KL, Lot 10, and dozens of malls
National Museum (Muzium Negara) — Malaysia's history from prehistoric to independence (RM5)
Merdeka Square — colonial heart of KL, Selangor Club and giant flagpole
Central Market (Pasar Seni) — heritage building for batik, pewter, and Malaysian crafts

essentials.gettingAround: MRT, LRT, Monorail, and KTM Komuter cover most attractions. Grab for point-to-point rides RM8-25.
essentials.language: Malay, English widely spoken, Cantonese in Chinatown

penang.title: Penang
penang.tagline: UNESCO heritage streets and Malaysia's food capital
penang.gettingThere: Fly into Penang International (PEN) — daily flights from KL (45 min), Singapore, Bangkok. Alternatively, KTM train to Butterworth then 10-min ferry to George Town.
highlights: George Town UNESCO World Heritage Site — largest collection of pre-war buildings in Southeast Asia
Penang street art by Ernest Zacharevic — life-size murals integrated with real street objects
Penang Hill cable car — 833m above sea level, cooler temperature and panoramic views (RM30)
Kek Lok Si temple complex — Southeast Asia's largest Buddhist temple, ornate seven-tier pagoda
Gurney Drive hawker centre — the best evening hawker spread in northern Malaysia
Clan Jetties — Chinese clan villages built on stilts over Penang Harbour since the 1800s
Penang char kway teow and assam laksa — two dishes that put Penang on the world food map
Penang National Park — 25km2 of rainforest with Monkey Beach and a turtle sanctuary

essentials.gettingAround: George Town is walkable. Grab for cross-island trips. Rental car or bicycle for the island.
essentials.language: Malay, English, Hokkien Chinese widely spoken

langkawi.title: Langkawi
langkawi.tagline: Duty-free island paradise in the Andaman Sea
langkawi.gettingThere: Fly into Langkawi International (LGK) — 45-min flight from KL or Penang. Ferry from Penang takes 2.5 hours (RM80-100).
highlights: Langkawi Cable Car and Sky Bridge — 2170m gondola to 708m peak, curved pedestrian bridge (RM55)
Pantai Cenang beach strip — Langkawi's liveliest beach with watersports and beach bars
Island hopping — Pulau Dayang Bunting lake, Pulau Beras Basah snorkeling, eagle feeding (RM45pp)
Eagle Square (Dataran Lang) — 12m bronze eagle statue, Langkawi's iconic landmark at the jetty
Duty-free shopping at Kuah town — chocolate, alcohol, and electronics at Singapore-beating prices
Tanjung Rhu secluded beach — mangrove-fringed bay on the remote north, Langkawi's most beautiful
Temurun Waterfall — 200m three-tier waterfall in the forest interior, short jungle walk from road
Kilim Karst Geoforest Park — UNESCO mangrove boat tour through limestone sea caves (RM120pp)

essentials.gettingAround: Rental car (RM70-120/day) or motorbike essential. Grab available but limited. Taxis for beach strips.

malacca.title: Malacca
malacca.tagline: Five centuries of colonial history in one city
malacca.gettingThere: Bus from KL TBS terminal takes 2 hours (RM12-15). No train or flight — bus is the only option. Also direct buses from Singapore (4 hrs, RM35).
highlights: A Famosa — the last remaining gatehouse of the 1511 Portuguese fortress that shaped Southeast Asian history
St. Paul's Hill and church ruins — Dutch and Portuguese era hilltop with panoramic harbour views
Jonker Street night market — Friday and Saturday nights, antiques and Nyonya food stalls (free entry)
Baba Nyonya Heritage Museum — Peranakan Chinese townhouse preserved exactly as it was in 1896 (RM16)
Stadthuys — Dutch colonial administrative building from 1650, now Malacca History Museum (RM15)
Peranakan cuisine — chicken rice balls, Nyonya laksa, and cendol along Jonker Street
Cheng Ho Cultural Museum — tribute to the 15th-century Muslim Chinese admiral who visited 7 times
Christ Church — Dutch Protestant church built 1753, still the most photographed building in Malacca

essentials.gettingAround: Historic city is walkable. Trishaw rides RM30-50. Grab for outer areas.
essentials.airport: Nearest is KL (2 hrs by bus)

cameron-highlands.title: Cameron Highlands
cameron-highlands.tagline: Cool tea plantations above the tropical heat
cameron-highlands.gettingThere: Bus from KL TBS takes 3.5-4 hours (RM25-35). Or bus from Ipoh (1.5 hrs, RM12). No train or flight. Cool mountain air at 1500m elevation — bring a light jacket.
highlights: BOH Tea Plantation and factory tour — Malaysia's most famous tea brand, free factory tour with panoramic views
Mossy forest trekking at Gunung Brinchang — 2032m peak, cloud forest dripping with moss and pitcher plants
Strawberry farms — pick your own strawberries at several farms along the main road (RM18/basket)
Bharat Tea Estate — 1000 acres of manicured tea rows, stunning for photography
Local night market at Brinchang — Tuesday and Wednesday evenings, corn, steamboat, and fresh produce
Sam Poh Buddhist Temple — elaborate hilltop temple complex with peacocks and fish ponds (free)
Butterfly Farm and Cactus Valley — popular tourist attraction complex near Kea Farm (RM12)
Rose Centre — 400 varieties of roses in highland gardens at Robinson Falls trailhead

essentials.gettingAround: Local buses connect main towns. Grab limited. Taxi or rental car for plantation visits.
essentials.airport: Nearest is Ipoh (Sultan Azlan Shah Airport, IPH) 1.5 hrs

kota-kinabalu.title: Kota Kinabalu
kota-kinabalu.tagline: Gateway to Borneo's highest peak and finest reefs
kota-kinabalu.gettingThere: Fly into Kota Kinabalu International (BKI) — direct flights from KL (2 hrs), Singapore, Manila, and several Chinese cities. AirAsia, Malaysia Airlines, and Scoot all serve the route.
highlights: Mount Kinabalu — 4,095m, highest peak in Southeast Asia, 2-day climb requires permit (RM300-800 guide fee)
Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park — 5 islands by 15-min boat, snorkeling among coral gardens (RM23 park fee)
Kinabatangan River wildlife cruise — best place in Borneo to see wild orangutans, proboscis monkeys, and pygmy elephants
Nexus Karambunai and Shangri-La Tanjung Aru beach resort strip — KK's finest beach hotels
Filipino Market sunset seafood — waterfront market with fresh grilled fish from RM15
Sabah State Museum — excellent collection of Sabah tribal artefacts and natural history (RM20)
Poring Hot Springs — Kinabalu Park annexe with jungle canopy walkway and sulphur pools (RM15)
Mantanani Island — remote pristine reef, dugong sightings possible, 2-hour boat from KK

essentials.gettingAround: Grab in city. Minibuses for day trips. Day tours for Mt Kinabalu and marine park.

sandakan.title: Sandakan
sandakan.tagline: Borneo's wildlife capital — orangutans in the wild
sandakan.gettingThere: Fly into Sandakan Airport (SDK) from Kota Kinabalu (40 min, RM80-150) or KL (2.5 hrs with connection). Or 5-hour bus from KK along the coastal highway (RM35).
highlights: Sepilok Orangutan Rehabilitation Centre — rescued orangutans fed at 10am and 3pm daily (RM30)
Kinabatangan River wildlife corridor — 560km river, one of the densest concentrations of wildlife in Borneo
Proboscis monkeys, pygmy elephants, crocodiles, and hornbills on Kinabatangan river cruises
Labuk Bay Proboscis Monkey Sanctuary — private sanctuary with multiple feeding platforms (RM60)
Turtle Islands Park — Pulau Selingan, witness sea turtle egg-laying on a permit-only overnight stay
Sandakan Death March Memorial — sobering tribute to WWII POWs who perished on the march to Ranau
Rainforest Discovery Centre at Sepilok — 280m canopy walkway and bird-rich forest (RM30)
Gomantong Caves — massive limestone cavern harvested for edible birds' nests (RM30)

essentials.gettingAround: Grab in town. Day tours and river cruises for wildlife areas.

kuching.title: Kuching
kuching.tagline: Sarawak's charming capital — culture, wildlife, and the world's largest cave
kuching.gettingThere: Fly into Kuching International (KCH) — daily flights from KL (1.5 hrs, RM80-200), Singapore (1.5 hrs), and Johor Bahru. Malaysia Airlines and AirAsia both serve this route.
highlights: Sarawak Cultural Village — 17-acre living museum of Borneo's indigenous tribes with dance performances (RM85)
Semenggoh Wildlife Centre — wild semi-habituated orangutans return to feeding platforms (RM10)
Bako National Park — 27km2 of rainforest, best place to see wild proboscis monkeys (RM20 entry + boat RM50)
Kuching Waterfront — colonial warehouses converted to restaurants and bars along the Sarawak River
Sarawak Museum — two buildings, one of the finest ethnographic museums in Asia (free)
Sarawak laksa and kolo mee — Kuching's two signature dishes, eaten for breakfast by locals
Sunday Market at Jalan Satok — Sarawak's wildest produce market, jungle ferns and exotic fruits from 6am
Cat Museum — Kuching means 'cat' in Malay; 4000 cat-related exhibits in the city hall annex (free)

essentials.gettingAround: Grab in city. Boat taxis across the river. Tour operator for national parks.

mulu.title: Mulu National Park
mulu.tagline: UNESCO caves and the bat exodus spectacle
mulu.gettingThere: Fly into Mulu Airport (MZV) via Miri (MYY) — MASwings flights from Kota Kinabalu or Kuching. No road access to the park. Plan at least 2 nights minimum.
highlights: Deer Cave — world's largest cave passage, 174m wide and 122m high, cathedral of stone
Bat exodus at dusk — millions of wrinkle-lipped bats spiral out of Deer Cave in a black tornado
Clearwater Cave — Southeast Asia's longest cave system at 227km, crystal-clear underground river
Lang Cave with stunning formations — stalactites, stalagmites, and flowstone in perfect condition
The Pinnacles trekking — 3-day expedition to see 45m limestone needles on Gunung Api (guide required RM700+)
Headhunter's Trail — 4-day jungle trek to Limbang by longboat through primary rainforest
Wind Cave — impressive passage with stalactites and resident cave fish in the underground river
Canopy skywalk — 480m of aerial walkways 25m above the forest floor near the park HQ (RM30)

essentials.gettingAround: Everything walkable within the park. Guided cave tours required (RM30-100/cave).
essentials.airport: Mulu Airport (MZV) — small MASwings prop planes only

tioman-island.title: Tioman Island
tioman-island.tagline: Pristine jungle-covered island with world-class diving
tioman-island.gettingThere: Ferry from Mersing on mainland (1.5-2.5 hrs, RM35-45). Or ferry from Singapore via Tanah Merah (4.5 hrs). Note: no ferries during monsoon season (Nov-Feb). Book accommodation and ferry in advance for weekends.
highlights: Juara Beach — tranquil east coast village accessible only by jungle trail or water taxi
Salang Beach — most popular backpacker base with the best reef right off the beach
Tekek to Juara jungle trek — 7km cross-island trail through primary rainforest (2-3 hrs)
PADI diving from RM300/dive — Tioman is on Time magazine's list of world's best islands for diving
Berjaya Tioman Resort — the island's only resort-scale hotel with tennis courts and golf (RM300+/night)
Renggis Island snorkeling — tiny island opposite Berjaya with guaranteed turtle sightings

essentials.gettingAround: Water taxis between villages RM5-20. Sea-crossing road connects some villages. Motorbike rental on the island.
essentials.airport: Closest is Mersing jetty on mainland

perhentian-islands.title: Perhentian Islands
perhentian-islands.gettingThere: Take a bus to Kuala Besut jetty (from KL ~5-6 hrs, RM40-60), then speedboat to the islands (30-40 min, RM70 return). Islands closed to tourists Nov-Feb (monsoon). Fly to Kota Bharu and taxi to Kuala Besut to save 3 hours from KL.
highlights: Long Beach on Perhentian Kecil — main backpacker strip with beach bars and dive shops
Coral Bay — calm sheltered bay on the west side of Kecil, perfect for beginner snorkeling
Green turtles and blacktip reef sharks — spotted on virtually every snorkel trip (RM30-50/trip)
PADI Open Water diving course from RM850 — some of Malaysia's best value dive instruction
Perhentian Besar — quieter large island with upmarket chalets and excellent house reef
Island hopping by speedboat — full loop of both islands RM50, guide included

essentials.gettingAround: Water taxis between beaches RM10-25. Most accommodation and snorkeling within walking distance.
essentials.airport: Closest is Kota Bharu (BKM) or fly to Kuala Terengganu (TGG)

taman-negara.title: Taman Negara
taman-negara.gettingThere: Bus from KL Pekeliling station to Jerantut (2.5 hrs, RM18), then local bus or taxi to Kuala Tahan jetty (1 hr), then boat across the river (RM1). Or book direct tour bus from KL guesthouses (4-5 hrs, RM65-80 including boat).
highlights: World's longest canopy walk — 530m of swaying suspension bridges 40m above the forest floor (RM5)
Night jungle walk — spot civets, nightjars, flying squirrels, and insects on a guided 2-hour walk (RM35)
Rapid shooting by boat on Sungai Tahan — exhilarating 2-hour downriver boat ride (RM50pp)
Bukit Teresek — 344m hill, 2-hour trek, panoramic views over the Tahan river valley (free)
Orang Asli cultural village visit — meet the indigenous Batek people, blowpipe demonstration (RM10)
Fishing on the Tembeling River — guided freshwater fishing for kerai and kelah (RM60 half-day)
Gua Telinga bat cave — 30-minute forest walk, 80m cave passage through roosting bats (free)
Multi-day jungle treks to Gunung Tahan — 9-day expedition to Peninsular Malaysia's highest peak (1939m)

essentials.gettingAround: Boat taxis on the river. Walking trails from Kuala Tahan village. Night hikes with guides.
essentials.airport: Nearest is KL (3-4 hrs by bus+boat)

ipoh.title: Ipoh
ipoh.tagline: Sleepy colonial city with Malaysia's best food scene
ipoh.gettingThere: KTM ETS train from KL takes 2 hours (RM28-35) — the fastest and most comfortable option. Or bus from KL TBS (2.5 hrs, RM15). Easy day trip or overnight from KL, also a natural stop on the KL-Penang corridor.
highlights: Ipoh Old Town murals and street art — iron rod sculptures and painted murals throughout the heritage quarter
Sam Poh Tong cave temple — 50 temple shrines inside a limestone cavern with turtle pond (free)
Kinta Valley limestone karst landscape — dramatic grey limestone towers rising from flat valley floor
White coffee at Nam Heong or Sin Yoon Loong — Ipoh invented this style, try it at the original shops (RM3)
Dim sum at Foh San or Ming Court — some of the best dim sum in Malaysia, arrive by 8am (RM30pp)
Concubine Lane and Panglima Lane — pedestrian shophouse lanes full of heritage cafes and boutiques
Kellie's Castle — unfinished Scottish planter's Indo-Moorish mansion from 1915, jungle-clad ruin (RM10)
Lost World of Tambun hot springs — waterpark with natural hot springs and tiger valley (RM66)

essentials.gettingAround: Old town walkable. Grab for outer areas. Bicycle rentals available.
essentials.airport: Sultan Azlan Shah Airport (IPH) — limited domestic flights
essentials.language: Malay, Cantonese Chinese widely spoken, English

george-town.title: George Town
george-town.tagline: Penang's UNESCO core — the best-preserved colonial streetscape in Asia
george-town.gettingThere: Fly into Penang International (PEN) then 30-min Grab (RM25) to George Town. Or arrive by KTM train to Butterworth then 10-min Rapid Penang ferry (RM1.20) to the Pengkalan Weld jetty, right in the heritage zone.
highlights: Ernest Zacharevic's iron rod street art murals — 'Boy on Bike' and 'Children on Bicycle' are must-finds
Khoo Kongsi — Malaysia's finest Hokkien clan house with extravagant carved pavilions (RM10)
Little India around Masjid Kapitan Keling — sari shops, jasmine garlands, and roti canai for RM2
Penang hawker food: char kway teow, assam laksa, rojak — the streets that launched a thousand food blogs
Chowrasta Market — bustling wet market and Penang nutmeg preserves on the edge of the heritage zone
Pinang Peranakan Mansion — museum of Straits-born Chinese opulence, 1000 antiques (RM25)
Fort Cornwallis — star-shaped 1786 British fort at the northeastern tip, free to walk around outside (RM20 inside)
Blue Mansion (Cheong Fatt Tze) — indigo-washed Hakka merchant mansion, guided tours daily (RM16)

essentials.gettingAround: The heritage core is fully walkable (30-min walk across). Free CAT bus along main streets.

johor-bahru.title: Johor Bahru
johor-bahru.tagline: Malaysia's southern gateway — shopping, food, and quick escapes from Singapore
johor-bahru.gettingThere: Direct flights from KL (50 min, RM60-120), Singapore, and Jakarta. Or KTM Komuter from Singapore Woodlands CIQ to JB Sentral (RM5, 5 min). 1-hour bus from Singapore Queen Street Terminal (RM5). The Causeway sees 300,000 crossings daily.
highlights: LEGOLAND Malaysia — the largest LEGOLAND in Asia, 40+ rides and LEGO-themed zones (RM185 adults)
Sultan Abu Bakar State Mosque — 1900 Victorian Moorish mosque on a hill above the Johor Strait (free, dress modestly)
Johor Bahru City Square and Komtar JBCC — back-to-back malls connected to JB Sentral, duty-free goods
Danga Bay waterfront park and Stulang Laut — sunset waterfront with seafood restaurants
Arulmigu Sri Rajamariamman Devasthanam — tallest Hindu temple gate (gopuram) in Malaysia (free)
Central Market (Pasar Besar Kota Raya) — three-storey market for local produce, textiles, and street food
Muar — 45-min drive north, the birthplace of Mee Bandung and Malaysia's best otak-otak fish cake
Kota Tinggi Waterfall — 37m falls and jungle pools 1 hour northeast of JB (RM5 entry)

essentials.gettingAround: Grab widely used. City is spread out — car or taxi needed for most attractions.
